description_long: This object is a handheld flashlight with a cylindrical body and a slightly flared head, typical of portable torches used for illumination. The body is predominantly black with subtle white accent lines running vertically along its length, giving it a sleek and modern appearance. A circular button or switch is visible on the side, likely serving as the power control, and there are some ridged sections near the base for improved grip. The head of the flashlight is wider than the handle, housing the reflective lens and bulb, which are not visible from these angles but are implied by the shape. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and ease of use, suitable for urban environments such as garages, sidewalks, or emergency kits. The materials appear to be mostly metal or hard plastic, with some internal components likely made of glass and electronic parts.
description: A sleek, black handheld flashlight with a cylindrical body, flared head, and a side button for operation. It features white accent lines and a ridged grip for secure handling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular head, vertical accent lines, and a central button on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the protruding button and the smooth cylindrical profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain cylindrical back with accent lines and no visible controls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the button visible, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
